'''Cort Davin''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Homeworld/Legends|native]] of [[Concord Dawn/Legends|Concord Dawn]]. He served as a [[Journeyman Protector/Legends|Journeyman Protector]] before he later joined the [[Cuy'val Dar]].

==History==
Cort Davin was born on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] planet of [[Concord Dawn/Legends|Concord Dawn]]<ref name="CSWE" /> located in the [[Mandalore sector/Legends|Mandalore sector]] of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref>

At some point, Davin joined the [[Journeyman Protector/Legends|Journeyman Protectors]],<ref name="GGAR" /> serving as one of Concord Dawn's respected [[Constable/Legends|constables]],<ref name="Revelation">''[[Legacy of the Force: Revelation]]''</ref> and acquired experience in [[Special forces/Legends|special forces]] operations.<ref name="GGAR" />

However, by the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[30 BBY/Legends|30 BBY]],<ref name="TZ">''[[Republic Commando: Triple Zero]]''</ref> Davin had ended his career with the Protectors and answered the call of [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]] to become one of the ''[[Cuy'val Dar]]'', a one-hundred-person group recruited by the veteran [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Soldier/Legends|soldier]] to train the [[Clone commando/Legends|clone commandos]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic's]] [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army]] on [[Kamino/Legends|Kamino]]. Though he was among the non-Mandalorian minority within the ''Cuy'val Dar'', like the others, he disappeared from the known galaxy at Fett's request, and trained the [[Clone cadet/Legends|clone soldiers]] assigned to him<ref name="GGAR" /> for close to a [[Standard decade/Legends|decade]], until their deployment at the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|Battle of Geonosis]].<ref name="TZ" />

Following the outbreak of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] at [[Geonosis/Legends|Geonosis]], a conflict waged between the Galactic Republic and the Separatists of the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]], Davin stayed on as [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] training [[Sergeant/Legends|sergeant]],<ref name="GGAR" /> assisting the [[Kaminoan/Legends|Kaminoan]] cloners with the instruction of new [[Advanced Recon Commando/Legends|Advanced Recon Commandos]] and [[Clone Commander/Legends|clone commanders]]<ref name="CSWE" /> as part of an [[ARC program/Legends|advanced learning program]] instituted by [[Alpha-class Advanced Recon Commando|ARC trooper]] [[Alpha (Advanced Recon Commando)|Alpha-17]]. 

Among Davin's trainees was [[Bacara/Legends|CC-1138]], known by the nickname "Bacara." From Davin, Bacara learned the ways of the Journeyman Protectors, coming to think of himself as a [[Law enforcement agency/Legends|enforcer of law]] and order. Davin also taught Bacara the [[Concordian/Legends|Concordian]] [[Language/Legends|dialect]] of [[Mando'a/Legends|''Mando'a'']], and came to view the early graduate of the [[Commander/Legends|commander]] training program<ref name="GGAR" /> as one of his most successful students.<ref name="CSWE" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|Remember back when you were a little kid, playing with your Star Wars action figures? Remember inventing new characters to share adventures alongside Luke and company? Well, this ex-Journeyman Protector named in the "GGAR" came from a character my friend created in his childhood. I couldn't help but sneak it in as a tribute to the fun we used to have playing Star Wars in the dirt with our 3 ¾ action figures.|[[Ryan Kaufman (real person)|Ryan Kaufman]]|<ref name="Blog">{{Blog|RyanKaufman|8|What's In a Name?}}</ref>}}
Cort Davin was first introduced to ''[[Star Wars]]'' [[canon]] in the ''[[Guide to the Grand Army of the Republic]]'', a ''[[Star Wars Insider]]'' article [[Author/Legends|authored]] by [[Karen Traviss]] and [[Ryan Kaufman (real person)|Ryan Kaufman]], and published in [[September]] of [[2005]] as part of the magazine's [[Star Wars Insider 84|eighty-forth issue]].<ref name="GGAR" />

In his blog on [[StarWars.com]], Ryan Kaufman revealed that he had named Davin after a character his childhood friend created in his youth, back when the two had played together with their ''Star Wars'' [[action figure]]s.<ref name="Blog" />
